title: Product Manager, Relevance and Personalization
score_rationale: Riley's background is a near-perfect domain match — hybrid AI search, search ranking, personalization, recommendation systems, and rigorous A/B experimentation at scale are all core to this Airbnb R&P role and deeply evidenced in the resume. The main friction point is the experience bar: the role explicitly requires 10+ years of PM experience, and Riley sits at 9 years total (with roughly 2 years at Senior PM level), making this a mild stretch on paper. The H-1B sponsorship requirement adds meaningful uncertainty given Airbnb's historically conservative immigration posture.
recommendation_reason: Domain alignment is exceptional — search ranking + personalization + conversational AI + experimentation at scale maps directly to Airbnb's R&P stack; the 1-year YOE gap is unlikely to be a hard filter.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years driving search relevance, personalization, and recommendation systems at scale — including hybrid AI search for 10M+ monthly users at Samsung and ML-driven catalog systems for 2M+ sellers at Amazon. Deep fluency in ML model evaluation, A/B experimentation design, and cross-functional alignment across guest and marketplace surfaces, with a track record of shipping technically complex systems that directly move booking and engagement metrics.
top_bullets: Led hybrid AI-powered search experience launch across 3 international marketplaces, partnering with AI researchers to optimize user engagement through A/B testing, resulting in 20% CTR increase and executive approval for global expansion

Partnered with ML engineers to improve search ranking using behavioral signals and catalog metadata, defining relevance metrics and experiment success criteria; improved precision@3 by 30% and 1st-result CTR by 45%

Developed a personalized recommendation API with data scientists using product, browsing, and purchase data, leveraged it to launch an add-ons page with UX designers and engineers, boosting AOV by 15%
gap_handling: The role asks for 10+ years; Riley has 9. Frame this proactively by leading with scope and scale of impact rather than YOE — 10M+ monthly users at Samsung and 2M+ sellers at Amazon signals readiness. In the cover letter, briefly acknowledge the "technically complex systems at scale" bar and map directly to the Samsung and Amazon examples. Avoid flagging the year gap; let the depth of ML/search experience carry the narrative.

title: Product Manager, Guest Discovery and Homepage
location: San Francisco CA, Los Angeles CA, New York NY
score_rationale: Riley's 9 years of PM experience maps cleanly onto Airbnb's 8+ year requirement, and her work on AI-powered search, product discovery, personalization, and recommendation systems at Samsung.com and Kolon directly mirrors what the homepage discovery role demands. Her track record of shipping consumer-facing discovery features at scale (10M+ monthly users, CTR lift, AOV growth) provides strong, direct evidence for most core requirements. The one honest gap is the consumer travel/marketplace domain — Riley's experience is in e-commerce/retail rather than two-sided travel marketplaces — and the role appears to lean slightly above a typical Senior PM toward a Lead/Staff level given the breadth of vertical ownership and executive alignment expectations.
recommendation_reason: Riley's AI search, discovery, and personalization background is a near-perfect archetype match; e-commerce discovery translates directly to Airbnb's homepage mission, and her scale/metrics profile is competitive.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years specializing in consumer discovery, AI-powered search, and personalization systems that drive measurable booking and engagement growth at scale. Led end-to-end launch of hybrid search, conversational AI, and recommendation experiences for 10M+ monthly users — bringing the same user-first discovery mindset to Airbnb's homepage and guest experience.
top_bullets: Defined the on-site search product vision and roadmap for Samsung.com, partnering with UX, engineering, and data science teams to enhance back-end logic and front-end UX/UI — increasing web navigation CSAT (NPS) by 20% QoQ and improving engagement for 10M+ monthly users.

Led hybrid AI-powered search experience launch across 3 international marketplaces, partnering with AI researchers to optimize user engagement through A/B testing, resulting in 20% CTR increase and executive approval for global expansion.
Built ML-driven personalized recommendation systems across the conversion funnel (PDP, Cart, Checkout), increasing AOV by 25% ($148 to $185) through model optimization and iterative A/B testing.
gap_handling: Riley has no direct two-sided marketplace or travel domain experience. In the cover letter and interview, explicitly connect e-commerce discovery (intent-sparse browsing, catalog ranking, seasonal demand) to Airbnb's supply/demand dynamics — they are structurally similar. Emphasize the international marketplace work at Samsung (3 markets) to demonstrate multi-market / multi-vertical thinking analogous to Airbnb's Homes + Experiences + Services verticals.

title: Senior Product Manager, Shopping Experience
score_rationale: Riley's background in e-commerce discovery, personalization, recommendation systems, A/B experimentation, and consumer-facing product ownership maps extremely well to Instacart's Shopping Experience team — particularly the preferred qualifications around search, recommendations, and marketplace experience. The 9 years of PM experience with measurable impact at Samsung.com (10M+ MAU) and Kolon (ML-driven recs, AOV lift, virtual try-on) directly satisfies the 6+ year requirement and the zero-to-one execution bar. The one notable gap is the Health/nutrition domain specificity — Instacart is explicitly building a regulated health product area, and Riley has no stated experience in dietary, wellness, or health data verticals, which prevents a higher score.
recommendation_reason: Strong consumer e-commerce + personalization + experimentation foundation checks nearly every box; health domain gap is bridgeable with framing and is listed only as a preferred (not required) qualification.
suggested_summary: Senior Product Manager with 9+ years building AI-powered discovery, personalization, and recommendation experiences for e-commerce platforms at Samsung.com and Kolonmall — driving measurable outcomes for 10M+ monthly users across search, PDP, and conversion funnels. Proven zero-to-one executor who translates deep customer insights into rigorous experimentation programs and scaled product launches, with a strong track record of shipping ML-driven features that improve engagement, AOV, and retention.
top_bullets: Built ML-driven personalized recommendation systems across the conversion funnel (PDP, Cart, Checkout), increasing AOV by 25% ($148 to $185) through model optimization and iterative A/B testing with data scientists, engineers, and UX designers.

Led end-to-end delivery of on-site conversational AI chat experience — from scope definition and conversation flow design to production launch — in partnership with marketing, UX, and engineering to support product discovery and comparison for users.

Defined the on-site search product vision and roadmap for Samsung.com, planning and leading a phased rollout with A/B testing across key funnels, increasing web navigation CSAT (NPS) by 20% QoQ and improving engagement for 10M+ monthly users.
gap_handling: The Health domain specialization is the clearest gap. In your cover letter and interview prep, bridge this by emphasizing that dietary filtering, nutrition-based discovery, and health-forward recommendations are fundamentally a personalization + discovery problem — exactly where your deepest experience lies. Point to your virtual try-on / size recommendation work (constraint-based filtering driving 70% adoption) as an analog for dietary/health-constraint-driven product design. If you have any personal connection to health, nutrition, or wellness as a user, weave that into your customer empathy narrative.

title: Senior Product Manager, AI (Customer Agent)
score_rationale: Riley's profile is a strong match on AI/ML product experience — conversational AI, LLM pipelines, search ranking, and recommendation systems all transfer well to Klaviyo's Customer Agent role. However, the role is squarely B2B SaaS (brand-facing configuration, onboarding flows, and agent tooling for business customers), while Riley's primary archetypes are AI Search and E-commerce Discovery — the B2B platform exposure at Amazon is real but narrower than what this role demands. Riley also lacks explicit RAG/agent architecture experience stated on the resume, and has no direct messaging-channel PM background (SMS, RCS, email automation), which is central to Klaviyo's multi-channel agent vision.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI/ML and conversational product credentials clear the bar, but limited B2B SaaS onboarding and multi-channel messaging experience creates a meaningful gap that requires targeted resume tailoring.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years shipping LLM-powered and conversational AI experiences — including an end-to-end on-site AI chat agent, hybrid search systems, and personalized recommendation pipelines serving 10M+ users. Proven track record collaborating with ML/data science teams to define agent behavior, build feedback loops, and drive measurable outcomes across complex B2C and B2B product surfaces.
top_bullets: Led end-to-end delivery of on-site conversational AI chat experience, from scope definition and conversation flow design to production launch in partnership with marketing, UX, and engineering to support product discovery and comparison for users
Defined an automated AEO strategy and pipeline to extract and structure catalog metadata from PIM into schema markup, boosting AI agent traffic by 8x and increasing conversion by 40% within 12 weeks

Automated product listing workflows for 2M+ B2B sellers by launching an ML-driven auto-fill system, reducing manual listing time by 80%, partnering with science and engineering to design multi-modal classification and data-mapping pipelines
gap_handling: Riley's resume doesn't explicitly mention RAG, agent architectures, or multi-channel deployment (SMS/RCS/email). Before applying, add a brief callout in the conversational AI bullet noting any retrieval or grounding mechanisms used, and consider adding a line about channel orchestration if the Samsung chat experience touched multiple surfaces. If RAG/LLM infrastructure was involved even peripherally, name it explicitly.

title: Staff Product Manager, Search Algorithms
location: New York City, NY; San Francisco, CA
score_rationale: Riley's background in AI-powered search, relevance ranking, hybrid search systems, and behavioral signal optimization at Samsung.com maps directly onto Faire's core Search Algorithms mission — the domain fit is strong. However, the role is titled Staff PM and signals "7+ years in complex Search systems at high-growth startups or as a founder," placing it one level above Riley's current PM III / Senior target; the seniority expectations are a stretch. The B2B wholesale marketplace context (retailer-facing) is adjacent but not a direct match to Riley's consumer e-commerce and DTC experience.
recommendation_reason: Strong search domain and AI/ML alignment, but the Staff-level seniority requirement and B2B wholesale marketplace context make it a stretch — tailoring is essential to close the gap.
suggested_summary: AI Search PM with 9+ years building and scaling search, discovery, and ranking systems — from hybrid AI search across international marketplaces to ML-driven relevance optimization driving measurable GMV impact. Proven ability to define search metrics, diagnose failure modes, and partner with ML engineers and data scientists to ship state-of-the-art search experiences for millions of users.
top_bullets: Partnered with ML engineers to improve search ranking using behavioral signals and catalog metadata, defining relevance metrics and experiment success criteria; improved precision@3 by 30% and 1st-result CTR by 45%

Led hybrid AI-powered search experience launch across 3 international marketplaces, partnering with AI researchers to optimize user engagement through A/B testing, resulting in 20% CTR increase and executive approval for global expansion

Defined the on-site search product vision and roadmap for Samsung.com, enhancing back-end logic and front-end UX/UI with phased rollout and A/B testing, increasing web navigation CSAT (NPS) by 20% QoQ for 10M+ monthly users
gap_handling: The Staff PM title and "7+ years on complex Search systems at a high-growth startup" bar is a stretch for Riley's current PM III / Senior level. Mitigate by leading with the scale and depth of the Samsung search work (10M+ users, multi-market rollout, precision@3 metrics) and framing the Amazon and Kolon tenures as compounding search/discovery experience. Avoid underselling the algorithm depth — explicitly name query segmentation, ranking signals, and offline/online metric design in the cover letter to match Faire's vocabulary.

title: Product Manager, API Growth
location: San Francisco, CA | New York City, NY | Seattle, WA
score_rationale: Riley has strong AI/ML product credentials, deep A/B testing and funnel optimization experience, and a compelling track record of shipping measurable growth outcomes — all directly relevant to Anthropic's API Growth PM role. However, the role specifically requires majority-growth experience on developer platforms, API products, or usage-based/technical products, and Riley's growth work has been primarily in consumer e-commerce discovery rather than developer-facing B2B/API funnels. The Amazon stint is the closest analog (B2B platform, 2M+ sellers, ML pipelines), but it was seller-tooling rather than API/developer growth. Riley clears the 6+ year bar at 9 years and the AI passion is genuine, but the developer platform growth gap is real and will be a filter at Anthropic's bar.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI/ML and growth credentials, but limited developer platform / API growth experience is a notable gap for this role's core requirement.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9 years of experience driving measurable growth across AI-powered search, discovery, and platform products at Samsung and Amazon — including ML-driven pipelines, A/B-tested funnel optimization, and self-serve workflows serving millions of users and sellers. Passionate about building at the frontier of AI and proven at translating complex model capabilities into products that drive acquisition, activation, and monetization at scale.
top_bullets: Automated product listing workflows for 2M+ B2B sellers by launching an ML-driven auto-fill system, reducing manual listing time by 80% — partnering with science and engineering to design multi-modal classification and data-mapping pipelines across 20+ categories

Defined the on-site search product vision and roadmap for Samsung.com, leading a phased rollout with A/B testing across key funnels, increasing NPS by 20% QoQ and improving engagement for 10M+ monthly users
Saved $1.4M in operational cost by enhancing self-serve workflow for B2B sellers, reducing manual support contacts through research-driven improvements across 5 dependent teams
gap_handling: The core gap is developer/API platform growth experience. To address this, Riley should explicitly reframe the Amazon role as a developer-facing platform story — sellers are API consumers in a sense, and the ML pipeline, self-serve workflows, and funnel instrumentation all map to developer growth primitives. Riley should also highlight any API integrations or developer touchpoints from the Samsung AEO pipeline and recommendation API work, and consider adding a brief note in the cover letter acknowledging the pivot from consumer/seller growth to developer growth while drawing concrete parallels.

title: Principal, Product Manager - AI / LLM
score_rationale: Riley's AI/ML product experience — spanning hybrid search, conversational AI, LLM pipelines, and RAG-adjacent work (AEO pipelines, recommendation APIs) — maps closely to the role's core focus on Copilot backend services and AI orchestration. The conversational AI chat experience at Samsung and the AEO metadata pipeline directly speak to the RAG and agent orchestration requirements. However, the role is explicitly titled "Principal PM" (a level Riley marks as "Avoid") and leans heavily into developer-platform and backend infrastructure domains (API platforms, SID error logs, B2B developer tooling) that differ from Riley's consumer/e-commerce discovery background. The 8+ years requirement is met, but the B2B developer/platform PM archetype is a meaningful gap.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI/LLM signal matches well, but the Principal title exceeds Riley's target seniority and the developer-platform/B2B backend focus is a meaningful archetype stretch from consumer e-commerce discovery.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years shipping LLM-powered systems — from conversational AI chat experiences and hybrid search pipelines to AEO metadata automation — that drive measurable engagement at scale. Proven track record orchestrating cross-functional AI product delivery across backend data pipelines, contextual personalization, and intelligent user assistance for audiences of 10M+ users.
top_bullets: Led end-to-end delivery of on-site conversational AI chat experience, from scope definition and conversation flow design to production launch in partnership with marketing, UX, and engineering to support product discovery and comparison for users

Defined requirements and led delivery of an automated AEO strategy and pipeline to extract and structure catalog metadata (product attributes, inventory, pricing) from PIM into schema markup, boosting AI agent traffic by 8x and increasing conversion by 40% within 12 weeks

Partnered with ML engineers to improve search ranking using behavioral signals and catalog metadata, defining relevance metrics and experiment success criteria; improved precision@3 by 30% and 1st-result CTR by 45%
gap_handling: The Principal title and B2B developer-platform focus are the two honest gaps here. For the title, Riley can lean into scope (10M+ users, multi-market launches, cross-org influence) to justify Principal-level impact without claiming the title directly. For the developer/B2B gap, the Amazon experience (2M+ B2B sellers, ML-driven listing pipelines, multi-modal classification) is the strongest bridge — lead with it when positioning for this role, and frame Samsung's internal tooling and AEO pipeline work as developer-adjacent backend product ownership.

title: Staff Product Manager, AI Personalization
score_rationale: Riley's background in AI-powered search, personalization, LLM pipelines, and conversational AI maps well to the core technical requirements — the Samsung AEO pipeline, recommendation APIs, and hybrid search work demonstrate genuine ML product depth. The domain alignment is strong: consumer AI, personalization, and discovery are Riley's home turf, and the Airbnb CS Platform is squarely a consumer AI + personalization play. However, this role is explicitly scoped as a Staff PM with 9+ years targeting platform/infrastructure ownership (retrieval architectures, RAG systems, multi-surface extensibility), which is a meaningful seniority stretch beyond Riley's Senior/PM III target. Riley also lacks explicit customer support / CX platform experience, which is the specific application domain here.
recommendation_reason: Strong technical and domain alignment with AI personalization, but the Staff PM seniority level and CS-platform-specific scope make it a competitive stretch — worth applying with carefully tailored positioning.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9 years of experience building personalization systems, hybrid search, and LLM-powered pipelines at consumer scale (Samsung.com, 10M+ monthly users). Proven track record of shipping full-stack AI products — from retrieval and ranking to conversational AI and recommendation APIs — that drive measurable outcomes across engagement, conversion, and user satisfaction.
top_bullets: Developed a personalized recommendation API with data scientists using product, browsing, and purchase data, leveraged it to launch an add-ons page with UX designers and engineers, boosting AOV by 15%

Defined requirements and led delivery of an automated AEO strategy and pipeline to extract and structure catalog metadata from PIM into schema markup, boosting AI agent traffic by 8x and increasing conversion by 40% within 12 weeks

Led end-to-end delivery of on-site conversational AI chat experience, from scope definition and conversation flow design to production launch in partnership with marketing, UX, and engineering to support product discovery and comparison for users
gap_handling: Riley lacks explicit customer support / CX platform experience — frame the conversational AI chat launch at Samsung as a support-adjacent discovery and resolution experience, and emphasize the AEO pipeline's multi-surface, extensible architecture to signal platform thinking. For the Staff-level gap, lean into the cross-functional scope at Samsung (10M+ users, global expansion, executive alignment) and the $1.4M cost-savings work at Amazon to demonstrate staff-caliber impact.

score_rationale: Riley's 9 years of PM experience — including AI-powered chat, conversational AI, personalized discovery, and help-surface-adjacent product work — maps meaningfully to this role's AI self-service and discovery-led redesign focus. However, the role is primarily a Help Center / CX support platform play and internal agent tooling owner, which is a domain Riley has not worked in directly; her experience skews toward e-commerce search, catalog, and recommendation systems rather than support deflection or knowledge base platforms. The Growth and AI angles are genuine overlaps, but the core product domain (customer support surfaces, KB strategy, deflection metrics) represents a real gap that would require deliberate positioning.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI/Growth/discovery transferability but no direct Help Center or CX support platform experience; worth applying with targeted framing if NYC hybrid works.
suggested_summary: Senior Product Manager with 9+ years building AI-powered discovery and conversational experiences at Samsung and Amazon, driving measurable outcomes for 10M+ monthly users. Proven ability to lead cross-functional teams through blue-sky platform redesigns — from AI chat to personalized product discovery — with a consistent focus on engagement, conversion, and self-service scale.
top_bullets: Led end-to-end delivery of on-site conversational AI chat experience, from scope definition and conversation flow design to production launch in partnership with marketing, UX, and engineering to support product discovery and comparison for users

Defined the on-site search product vision and roadmap for Samsung.com, partnering with UX, engineering, and data science teams to enhance back-end logic and front-end UX/UI — increasing web navigation CSAT score (NPS) by 20% QoQ and improving engagement for 10M+ monthly users

Defined requirements and led delivery of an automated AEO strategy and pipeline to extract and structure catalog metadata from PIM into schema markup, boosting AI agent traffic by 8x and increasing conversion by 40% within 12 weeks
gap_handling: Riley has no direct Help Center, support deflection, or CustOps tooling experience — address this head-on in the cover letter by drawing a direct parallel: her conversational AI chat launch at Samsung is functionally similar to an AI-powered Help Center (self-service resolution, conversation flow design, user unblocking). Frame the AEO pipeline and structured content work as analogous to knowledge base architecture. Acknowledge the domain shift but position it as a deliberate growth move into a high-impact, AI-forward surface.

title: Staff Product Manager, Lyft AI Platform & Marketplace Applications
score_rationale: Riley's AI/ML product background, strong experimentation chops, and hands-on work with LLM pipelines (AEO, conversational AI) are genuinely relevant to Lyft's AI Platform org. However, this role is squarely a Staff-level Platform/Infrastructure PM focused on agentic systems and internal AI tooling — Riley's experience is predominantly in consumer-facing search and discovery, not platform/developer-tooling products or marketplace dynamics in a rideshare context. The requirement for deep agentic system knowledge, multi-agent orchestration, and internal platform evangelism represents a meaningful gap compared to Riley's resume.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI/ML foundation and technical credibility are assets, but the Staff-level seniority, platform/infra PM archetype, and agentic systems depth are all stretches for Riley's current positioning.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years shipping AI-powered systems at scale — from hybrid search ranking and LLM-driven pipelines to conversational AI and recommendation platforms — across Samsung, Amazon, and high-growth e-commerce. Experienced leading technically complex AI products end-to-end with cross-functional teams of engineers, data scientists, and UX, with a track record of measurable impact (8x AI agent traffic, 10M+ monthly users, $1.4M cost savings).
top_bullets: Defined requirements and led delivery of an automated AEO strategy and pipeline to extract and structure catalog metadata from PIM into schema markup, boosting AI agent traffic by 8x and increasing conversion by 40% within 12 weeks

Automated product listing workflows for 2M+ B2B sellers by launching an ML-driven auto-fill system, reducing manual listing time by 80%, partnering with science and engineering to design multi-modal classification and data-mapping pipelines

Led end-to-end delivery of on-site conversational AI chat experience, from scope definition and conversation flow design to production launch in partnership with marketing, UX, and engineering to support product discovery and comparison
gap_handling: The biggest gap is platform/internal-tooling PM experience and agentic systems depth. Avoid overstating hands-on coding experience with PyTorch/Keras — those are listed as "familiar with caution" on the resume.

title: Product Manager, Consumer
location: San Francisco, CA | New York City, NY
score_rationale: Riley has strong AI/ML product credentials, a proven track record shipping search, discovery, and conversational AI at scale, and 9 years of PM experience that comfortably clears the 8-year bar. However, the role explicitly calls for a "former consumer founder" who spikes on 0-to-1 product creation — Riley's background is primarily 1-to-N optimization and feature scaling at established companies (Samsung, Amazon, Kolon), not greenfield consumer product invention or startup founding. The hybrid SF/NYC in-office requirement (25%+) is manageable from Bellevue but adds friction, and the $385K–$460K comp signals this is a very senior, high-bar hire where the founder-pedigree filter is real.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI/consumer domain fit and solid credentials, but the "former founder" and 0-to-1 product invention requirement is a real gap that makes this a competitive stretch without startup experience.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years shipping consumer-facing AI experiences — including conversational AI chat, hybrid search, and personalized discovery systems serving 10M+ monthly users — with a track record of translating emerging model capabilities into products people love. Deeply hands-on across the full product lifecycle from vision and prototyping through scaled global launch, with a bias for creative, low-cost experimentation to validate product-market fit fast.
top_bullets: Led end-to-end delivery of on-site conversational AI chat experience, from scope definition and conversation flow design to production launch, supporting product discovery and comparison for millions of users on Samsung.com

Defined an automated AEO strategy and pipeline to extract and structure catalog metadata into schema markup, boosting AI agent traffic by 8x and increasing conversion by 40% within 12 weeks — a 0-to-1 initiative taken from concept to production

Launched hybrid AI-powered search experience across 3 international marketplaces, partnering with AI researchers to optimize engagement through A/B testing, resulting in 20% CTR increase and executive approval for global expansion
gap_handling: Riley has no stated startup founding experience — the single biggest filter for this role. To address this, the cover letter should reframe the AEO pipeline and conversational AI chat launches as 0-to-1 entrepreneurial bets driven by Riley's initiative, not top-down roadmap items. If Riley has any side projects, independent AI product experiments, or early-stage advisory work, those should be surfaced prominently. Leaning into hands-on prototyping with Claude or other AI tools (per the JD's "Claude Code" signal) would also help close the founder-proxy gap.

title: Product Manager, Monetization
location: San Francisco, CA | New York City, NY | Seattle, WA
score_rationale: Riley's AI/ML product background and e-commerce conversion/funnel work shows strong transferable skills, but this role is squarely a Growth/Monetization PM archetype — specifically subscription pricing, packaging, free-to-paid conversion, and consumption-based billing — which is not Riley's primary archetype. The Samsung and Kolon experience includes A/B testing, AOV optimization, and recommendation-driven conversion, but lacks direct ownership of subscription monetization, pricing models, or upgrade funnel mechanics for a SaaS or consumer subscription business. The domain is Consumer AI at Anthropic, which aligns with Riley's target, but the PM type (Growth/Monetization) is a meaningful stretch from Riley's Search & Discovery core.
recommendation_reason: AI domain and analytical chops align, but direct subscription monetization and pricing/packaging ownership are missing — a core requirement here.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years driving measurable revenue and engagement growth through data-driven experimentation, funnel optimization, and ML-powered product experiences at Samsung and Amazon. Proven track record owning cross-functional growth initiatives — from conversion funnel optimization and personalized recommendations to A/B testing at scale — with a passion for building safe, beneficial AI products that deliver real user value.
top_bullets: Built ML-driven personalized recommendation systems across the conversion funnel (PDP, Cart, Checkout), increasing AOV by 25% ($148 to $185) through model optimization and iterative A/B testing
Streamlined product discovery funnels by launching category-specific finder experiences and optimizing taxonomy, increasing PDP view rate per visit by 45% and reducing bounce rate by 50%
Defined success metrics for search engagement and led instrumentation strategy; leveraged behavioral insights to iterate search experiences, increasing search success rate by 15% and CTR by 25% QoQ
gap_handling: The biggest gap is direct ownership of subscription monetization mechanics — pricing tiers, packaging, free-to-paid conversion funnels, and consumption-based billing. Riley should explicitly frame the Kolon onboarding/retention work (first purchase rate +30%, re-purchase rate +15%) and AOV optimization as monetization outcomes, and consider adding a brief cover letter note acknowledging the growth PM pivot while highlighting the AI domain expertise as a differentiator at Anthropic specifically.

title: Senior Product Manager - Data Agent
score_rationale: Riley's AI/ML product chops — hybrid search, LLM pipelines, NLP, AEO, and conversational AI at Samsung — are directly relevant to the AI-powered query and governance aspects of this role. However, the core domain requirement is BI/analytics/data warehouse (semantic layers, modern data stack, BI tools like Looker/dbt), which is not represented in Riley's resume; her experience skews toward e-commerce search and discovery rather than enterprise analytics infrastructure. The SQL skill and data science collaboration experience provide a bridge, but there's a meaningful gap in BI/data stack domain credibility that will be apparent to Datadog's hiring team.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI product DNA and 0-to-1 delivery track record, but lacks the BI/data warehouse/semantic layer domain depth that is a stated core requirement for this role.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years shipping LLM-powered pipelines, conversational AI, and query understanding systems at Samsung and Amazon. Proven 0-to-1 delivery track record — from defining governance frameworks and eval metrics to launching AI-driven experiences for 10M+ users — with hands-on SQL and data instrumentation skills that translate directly to building trustworthy AI analytics products.
top_bullets: Defined requirements and led delivery of an automated AEO strategy and pipeline to extract and structure catalog metadata from PIM into schema markup, boosting AI agent traffic by 8x and increasing conversion by 40% within 12 weeks

Led end-to-end delivery of on-site conversational AI chat experience, from scope definition and conversation flow design to production launch in partnership with marketing, UX, and engineering to support product discovery and comparison for users

Led cross-functional team to design a unified customer data platform (CDP), integrating offline (POS, SAP CRM) and online behavioral data to create a single customer view in BigQuery, defining requirements for analytics tool integration (Google Analytics, Looker, Tableau), driving a 45% increase in marketing campaign ROI
gap_handling: The biggest gap is BI/data stack domain knowledge — Datadog will look for candidates who can speak fluently about semantic layers (dbt metrics, LookML, Cube), data warehouses (Snowflake, BigQuery, Redshift), and BI tools (Looker, Tableau, Mode). Mitigate this by: (1) explicitly calling out BigQuery and Tableau work in the CDP bullets, (2) studying the modern data stack enough to speak to it in a screen, and (3) leaning hard into the LLM eval, governance, and 0-to-1 angles where Riley is genuinely strong. Also note: this role is NYC-based with a hybrid requirement — Riley is in Bellevue, WA, which is a real logistical hurdle unless she's open to relocating.

title: Senior Product Manager, Okta for AI Agents
location: Bellevue, Washington; Chicago, Illinois; New York, New York; Washington, DC
score_rationale: Riley has strong AI/ML product credentials — shipping LLM pipelines, conversational AI, and search systems — which covers the "AI-adjacent" half of this role well. However, the core requirement is deep Identity & Access Management expertise (OAuth2, OIDC, SAML, Zero Trust, agent authentication/authorization), a domain Riley has no evident background in. This is a developer/security-platform PM role, not a consumer AI or e-commerce discovery role, so the archetype fit is meaningful but partial. The AEO pipeline and API design experience (recommendation API, structured data) hint at technical depth, but IAM is a hard, specialized requirement that can't easily be papered over.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI/LLM product credibility covers ~half the role, but zero IAM/OAuth/OIDC/security experience is a significant gap for the core requirement of this position.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years shipping LLM-powered systems — from conversational AI and hybrid search to structured data pipelines and recommendation APIs — at Samsung and Amazon scale. Experienced in defining technical protocols, partnering with engineers on system design, and launching "zero-to-one" AI products for millions of users; eager to apply this foundation to securing the next generation of AI agent identities at Okta.
top_bullets: Led end-to-end delivery of on-site conversational AI chat experience, from scope definition and conversation flow design to production launch in partnership with marketing, UX, and engineering to support product discovery and comparison for users

Defined requirements and led delivery of an automated AEO strategy and pipeline to extract and structure catalog metadata from PIM into schema markup, boosting AI agent traffic by 8x and increasing conversion by 40% within 12 weeks

Developed a personalized recommendation API with data scientists using product, browsing, and purchase data, leveraged it to launch an add-ons page with UX designers and engineers, boosting AOV by 15%
gap_handling: Be upfront in your cover letter: acknowledge you're coming from the AI product side rather than the IAM/security side, and position yourself as the "AI-native PM who understands agentic systems from the inside." Reference your multi-agent workflow familiarity and API design experience explicitly. Consider quickly studying OAuth2/OIDC flows and Zero Trust basics (Auth0 docs, Okta developer blog) so you can speak credibly to the protocol layer in an interview. If you get a screen, lean hard into the AEO pipeline (AI agent traffic) and conversational AI work — those are the closest signals to "building for agents."

title: Product Manager, Claude Code
score_rationale: Riley's AI/ML product background and experience shipping LLM-driven pipelines (conversational AI, hybrid search, AEO pipelines) are genuinely relevant to Anthropic's AI-first culture. However, the role has a hard requirement of 1+ years as a professional software engineer — Riley's resume shows no engineering tenure, and the role centers on developer tooling (CLI, coding agents) for a highly technical user base, which is a material gap. Domain alignment is strong on AI but weak on dev tools specifically; Riley's work is in e-commerce discovery and consumer AI, not developer-facing products.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI product credibility and sponsorship match, but missing the required engineering background and dev tools experience that Anthropic explicitly prioritizes for this role.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years shipping LLM-driven systems — from agentic pipelines and conversational AI to hybrid search and structured data extraction — with a hands-on approach to working alongside ML researchers and engineers. Passionate about translating cutting-edge model capabilities into intuitive, high-impact product experiences for technical and non-technical users alike.
top_bullets: Defined requirements and led delivery of an automated AEO strategy and pipeline to extract and structure catalog metadata from PIM into schema markup, boosting AI agent traffic by 8x and increasing conversion by 40% within 12 weeks

Led end-to-end delivery of on-site conversational AI chat experience, from scope definition and conversation flow design to production launch in partnership with marketing, UX, and engineering to support product discovery and comparison for users

Automated product listing workflows for 2M+ B2B sellers by launching an ML-driven auto-fill system, reducing manual listing time by 80%; partnered with science and engineering to design multi-modal classification and data-mapping pipelines
gap_handling: The 1-year professional engineering requirement is the biggest filter. Riley should address this directly — if there is any coding, scripting, or engineering-adjacent work (e.g., Python, SQL, API design, pipeline work alongside engineers), those should be surfaced explicitly. Even coursework from the Purdue MSBA program in data engineering or modeling could be noted. If Riley has no engineering tenure whatsoever, this is a likely screen-out criterion and the application should be accompanied by a strong referral or a cover letter that reframes deep technical collaboration as a proxy.

title: Principal Product Manager, AI & Automation
score_rationale: Riley's AI/ML product experience — hybrid search, LLM pipelines, conversational AI, and recommendation systems — directly maps to several of Okta's requirements around shipping AI/ML products in production and building with LLMs. However, this is a Principal PM role requiring 10+ years of experience (Riley has 9), and the domain is enterprise identity/IAM — a significant departure from Riley's e-commerce/search/discovery background. The "Customer Zero" framing means building internal enterprise AI tools (agents, copilots, workflow automation for IT/HR/Finance/GTM), not consumer-facing search or discovery products — a meaningful archetype mismatch.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI/ML credentials and shipping track record are compelling, but enterprise identity domain gap, Principal-level seniority stretch, and SF location make this a competitive long shot worth tailoring for.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years shipping LLM-driven pipelines, conversational AI, and intelligent automation systems at scale — including a production conversational AI experience and AEO pipeline that drove 8x AI agent traffic growth. top_bullets: Led end-to-end delivery of on-site conversational AI chat experience, from scope definition and conversation flow design to production launch, supporting product discovery and comparison for 10M+ monthly users
Defined an automated AEO strategy and pipeline to extract and structure catalog metadata into schema markup, boosting AI agent traffic by 8x and increasing conversion by 40% within 12 weeks

Partnered with ML engineers to improve search ranking using behavioral signals and catalog metadata, defining relevance metrics and experiment success criteria; improved precision@3 by 30% and 1st-result CTR by 45%
gap_handling: The biggest gap is domain: Okta's "Customer Zero" team builds internal enterprise AI tools for IT/HR/Finance workflows — not consumer search or e-commerce. In your cover letter and resume summary, reframe your Samsung conversational AI and AEO pipeline work as enterprise-grade AI automation. Emphasize your cross-functional leadership with IT, data, and operations-side stakeholders (e.g., the CDP build with SAP CRM/POS integration at Kolon, or the Amazon seller tooling work) to demonstrate enterprise workflow sensibility. Also, the Principal title requires 10+ years — acknowledge your 9 years confidently by leading with depth of AI shipping experience over raw tenure.

title: Senior Product Manager, Okta for AI Agents
score_rationale: Riley has strong AI/ML product credentials — LLM pipelines, conversational AI, agentic workflows, and multi-agent signals — that are directionally relevant. However, this role is squarely in enterprise identity security (IAM, OAuth2/OIDC, SAML, Zero Trust), a domain Riley has no documented experience in, which is a hard requirement, not a nice-to-have. Additionally, the role is developer-platform / B2B enterprise, a departure from Riley's consumer/e-commerce core. The LLM and AI agent awareness partially bridges the gap, but the identity infrastructure expertise is a meaningful missing piece.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI/LLM product background is genuinely relevant, but the IAM/OAuth/OIDC domain expertise is a hard requirement Riley can't currently satisfy — needs honest gap acknowledgment.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years shipping LLM-powered pipelines, conversational AI, and multi-agent workflows at Samsung and Amazon, reaching 10M+ monthly users. top_bullets: Led end-to-end delivery of on-site conversational AI chat experience, from scope definition and conversation flow design to production launch, supporting AI-driven product discovery for 10M+ monthly users

Defined requirements and led delivery of an automated AEO strategy and pipeline to extract and structure catalog metadata into schema markup, boosting AI agent traffic by 8x and increasing conversion by 40% within 12 weeks

Before applying, Riley should invest a few hours in self-study — read Okta's developer docs on OAuth2/OIDC flows, skim the emerging MCP (Model Context Protocol) spec, and reference any API design or token-based auth work (even indirectly) in the resume. In the cover letter, frame the AEO pipeline and multi-agent workflow familiarity as a direct bridge to AI agent governance, and be upfront that the identity security domain is a learning edge — Okta may value the fresh AI product instincts over a traditional IAM background for this zero-to-one charter.

title: Senior Product Manager, Project & Task Experience

The "complex data models, visualizations, and human-centric UX for enterprise" emphasis is a partial gap — Riley has PDP and funnel UX work, but no enterprise productivity SaaS, complex data relationship modeling, or org-level collaboration tooling experience. Riley has zero ads/ad tech experience; her background is in search, discovery, e-commerce, and personalization. Additionally, this is a people-manager role requiring a track record of hiring/mentoring PMs, which is explicitly outside Riley's stated target seniority (she wants to avoid Director/GPM titles). The domain mismatch (ads marketplace vs. search/discovery/AI) and level mismatch (GPM vs. Senior PM) are both fundamental disqualifiers.
recommendation_reason: Double mismatch: wrong domain (ad tech/ads marketplace vs. search & discovery) and wrong level (Group PM / people manager vs. target IC Senior PM).
